---
artifact_id: ESE111-CH04-NOTES
pair_id: ESE111-CH04-NOTES
lang: en
pair_revision: 0.1.0
semantic_id: ESE111-CH04-NOTES
id: ESE111-CH04-NOTES-en
resource_type: professor-notes
language: en
paired_with: ESE111-CH04-NOTES-fr
version: 0.1.0
lifecycle: drafting
access: staff
outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
technical_contract:
  outcomes: [ESE111-CH04-LO01, ESE111-CH04-LO02, ESE111-CH04-LO03, ESE111-CH04-LO04, ESE111-CO01, PLO-01, PLO-02, PLO-07, PLO-08]
  equations: [KCL signed current sum equals zero, KVL signed loop voltage sum equals zero, v equals R times i, power equals v times i]
  values: {source_v: 6.0, r1_ohm: 10000, r2_ohm: 22000, r3_ohm: 33000, r4_baseline_ohm: 15000, r4_redesign_ohm: 22000}
  safety_limits: {source_max_v: 6.1, current_limit_max_a: 0.02, dmm_current_input_permitted: false}
  expected_ranges: {baseline_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.087, 1.155], redesign_tp_b_simulated_v: [1.412, 1.500], source_current_simulated_a: [0.000229, 0.000244]}
  rubric_total: 28
  answer_key_digest: pending-restricted-store
  figure_ids: [ESE111-CH04-FIG-001]
---

# Professor notes — Kirchhoff models

## Instructional intent and evidence

This unit is the first complete rehearsal of “physical circuit ↔ schematic ↔
equations ↔ simulation ↔ measurement ↔ requirement.” Its central move is not
solving two equations; it is making every term accountable to a branch, then using
a residual to decide what to test. Required evidence is initial prediction, signed
model, independent checks, instrument branch, hypothesis update, and verified R4
decision. Downstream uses include dividers/sensors, formal nodal analysis,
uncertainty, Thévenin, and M1 diagnosis.

## Preparation and current evidence boundary

Allow 35 minutes to review/print and 15 minutes for a live setup plus 5 minutes
reset after it is qualified. The repository oracle passes nominal, KCL, power,
loading, tolerance, and design tests. No physical golden data, approved expected
range, second-instructor rehearsal, or learner pilot exists yet. Do not describe
the setup as tested until the QA record links attributable evidence for the exact
source, meters, component lots, minimal/standard paths, and substitutes.

Prepare slides/static PDF, board plan, schematic text, PREP-A, exit slips,
remediation map, approved glossary, accessible response routes, and the demo safe
stop. Controlled solution, current items, assessment variants, and blind faults
must be resolved through their opaque references, never copied into public notes.

## Questions and expected responses

- Prediction: $V_A>V_B>0$; $I_1=I_2+I_3$. Ask “where could charge accumulate?”
  before telling.
- Estimate: current <0.6 mA, nodes inside rails. Reject an impossible solver output.
- Loading: attaching finite $R_M$ lowers B and adds $I_M$. Ask which measurement
  could disconfirm loading rather than calling the meter “bad.”
- Diagnosis: a test is good only if outcomes separate hypotheses; replacement is
  not an observation.
- Transfer: increasing R4 raises B but changes current and tolerance margin. A
  simulation pass proposes a design; physical rerun verifies it.

## Misconceptions to surface

| Model | Elicit | Distinguishing evidence | Route |
|---|---|---|---|
| current is used up | ask whether $I_1=I_2$ | signed TP-A inventory | REM-signs/model |
| voltage belongs to a point | omit COM in a statement | report same branch under another reference | REM-reference |
| KVL means equal voltages | offer equal-drop distractor | signed loop sum | faded loop example |
| negative means wrong | reverse one arrow | same physics, negative result | REM-signs |
| meter is invisible | compare unloaded/1 MΩ | branch current equals KCL residual | REM-loading |
| digits equal accuracy | show fine display/no spec | accuracy + loading + repeatability | REM-uncertainty |
| simulation is hardware proof | show passing oracle | ask what was never connected | evidence labels |
| replace first | present three hypotheses | predicted test outcomes | REM-debug |

## Teaching, terminology, and access decisions

Use the four-zone board plan in the studio file. Keep reference arrows and the
original prediction visible. In English say *voltage*; in French prefer *tension
électrique*, with *courant électrique*, *nœud*, *branche*, *loi des nœuds*, and
*loi des mailles*. Decimal commas occur in French prose, but code/CSV stay with dots.

Do not rely on red/green, probe color, animation, or projected detail. Narrate node
connections, provide the SVG long description and print table, and accept equivalent
keyboard/oral/directing-manipulation routes. In a large class, use kits only after
inspection stations and role cards; every learner still submits an individual
prediction, test decision, and exit response.

The generic repair fixture is the portable context. Do not improvise claims about
local needs or lamp/sensor performance while the M01 brief is unapproved. Students
may discuss how a threshold would matter in a real system only as a hypothetical
requirement with explicit missing evidence.

## Recovery and what not to cut

On equipment failure, stop/isolate/verify zero, preserve configuration/readings,
then move to the static pair and board branch. On power/internet loss, all sources,
model output, prepared data, and print files are local. On projector failure use
the board sequence; on instructor absence the replacement follows the minute plan
but must not run unapproved hardware. Never cut the prediction, signed references,
loading branch, discriminating test, safety checkpoint, design verification, or exit.

## Calibration and follow-through

Level 3 evidence names assumptions, retains raw data, considers loading, chooses a
test that can fail its hypothesis, and states design margin/limit. Level 2 usually
has correct arithmetic but weak uncertainty or diagnosis; Level 1 imitates steps.
Safety/integrity are conjunctive. Use changed parameters for reassessment. Classify
exit tickets into lab-ready, REM-loading, REM-debug, REM-reference/algebra, and
REM-dmm. Record actual timing, learner wording, access issue, apparatus behavior,
defect/owner, and paired correction. Second-instructor rehearsal evidence belongs
in the E3 record, not as an unchecked claim here.
